rulings.Case Title: MAIN STREET BUSINESS LOANS, LLC VS. MARKET VENTURES CORP. A CORPORATION DBA GOLD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 15, 2015, Line 1, PETITIONER MAIN STREET BUSINESS LOANS, LLC'S Hearing On Petition And Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award. Grant, no opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: FEI SU VS. SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Inspection Demand, Set One, And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 15, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT JAVAD MIRSAIDI'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Inspection Demand, Set One, And Monetary Sanctions. The motion is granted and denied in part. The court grants the motion to compel further special interrogatory responses to special interrogatories 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, 20, 23, 26, 28, 32, 35, 38, 40, 42, 43, 45, 48, 49, and 50. Plaintiff must provide a privilege log as to those documents that it asserts are privileged. With regard to the text messages, plaintiff shall make the CD available for inspection. Plaintiff shall provide further responses and the CD by August 7, 2015. The court denies the sanctions request because the moving party did not properly notice that request. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: TONY LAU VS. ALEXEY VEREIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defendant Alexey Verein To Provide Answers Ot Interrogatories; And Responses To Request For Produciton Of Document;And To Appear For Deposition With Documents;And For Sanctionss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, July 15, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF TONY LAU's Motion To Compel Defendant Alexey Verein To Provide Answers to Interrogatories, And Responses To Request For Production Of Document, And To Appear For Deposition With Documents and For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's unopposed motion to compel code-complaint responses to interrogatories and document request without objections, and to appear for oral deposition, is granted. All written responses shall be served within fifteen days of the date of service of this order with Notice of Entry. Plaintiff's deposition shall be held within thirty days of service of said Order. Sanction in the sum of $750.00 shall be paid by defendant within the aforesaid thirty days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ADON NETWORK, LLC VS. ONESCREEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set One And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Sanctions Against Defendant Onescreen, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, July 15, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ADON NETWORK, LLC's Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set One And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Sanctions Against Defendant Onescreen, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories 15.1, 50.2, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5 is granted. The defendant shall provide all information known to it as of the date of its response. The Court intends to grant plaintiff's motion to compel production of multiple documents which are the subject of the document request. A hearing is required to resolve issues pertaining to a protective order, rulings on objections, and preparation of a privilege log. The Court finds an award of monetary sanctions to be appropriate. Sanctions are requested in plaintiff's Notice of Motion, however, plaintiff's counsel has failed to provide a declaration "setting forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought". See CCP 2023.040. The issue of failure to provide a formal declaration with facts as to the computation of the amount of damages shall be addressed by both sides at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)
